legacy_element: JSP pages with scriptlets and direct SQL (e.g. reports.jsp, hours.jsp)
reason: Presentation, DB access and business logic mixed in JSP scriptlets — hard to test and maintain.
trade_off: Large migration effort; UI redesign and routing changes; potential short-term regressions.
rediscovery_ref: [rediscovery.md](rediscovery.md) - Business Rules & Integration Inventory: JSPs and direct DB access

legacy_element: Direct JDBC inside JSPs (hard-coded dbUrl in reports.jsp)
reason: Bypasses app datasource management and duplicates connection logic; security and maintainability risk.
trade_off: Moderate refactor to move SQL into DAOs/services; immediate improvement in testability.
rediscovery_ref: [rediscovery.md](rediscovery.md) - Integration Inventory: Embedded Derby and JSP direct access

legacy_element: Embedded Apache Derby file DB usage (jdbc:derby:./data/bigbadmonolith)
reason: File-based embedded DB unsuitable for multi-instance/cloud deployments and scaling.
trade_off: Increased ops cost for managed RDBMS but gains availability, backups, and scale.

reason: Useful for local dev but unsafe/noisy in production startup; risks seeding non-prod data.
trade_off: Keep for dev/test only; ensure production disables it; maintain separate dev tooling.

reason: Joda-Time is legacy; `java.time` is standard in modern Java and better supported.
trade_off: Refactor effort to convert types and persistence mapping; validate timezone/format differences.

reason: Unstructured console logging hinders observability; structured logging recommended.
trade_off: Integrate SLF4J/logback; update logging config and deployments; minimal code changes.

legacy_element: Hard-coded dev credential and basicRegistry in server.xml (user1/password)
reason: Secrets in config are unsafe for managed/cloud deployments; use secrets management or external identity.
trade_off: Requires secrets management and potential auth flow changes; improves security.

legacy_element: DB schema creation logic embedded in app (inline DDL in ConnectionManager/LibertyConnectionManager)
reason: Ad-hoc runtime DDL is brittle for repeatable migrations; use a migration tool (Flyway/Liquibase).
trade_off: One-time migration to adopt tooling and remove inline DDL; better schema control.

legacy_element: Inconsistent numeric handling for financials (BigDecimal in services vs double in JSP/SQL)
reason: Inconsistent types cause rounding/precision risk in billing calculations.
trade_off: Refactor reporting to use BigDecimal end-to-end; small API/query changes required.

legacy_element: Per-DAO non-transactional operations (no explicit transaction manager)
reason: Multi-step operations lack transaction boundaries which can lead to partial updates.
trade_off: Introduce transaction manager (JTA/Spring) and adjust DAO usage; increased testing and integration effort.

reason: Filesystem-anchored assumptions are cloud-hostile (ephemeral instances, containers).
trade_off: Move to platform-managed storage or managed DB; requires ops and config changes.
rediscovery_ref: [rediscovery.md](rediscovery.md) - Integration Inventory: derby paths & server vars

legacy_element: Business logic duplicated in JSPs (report SQL + presentation) vs service logic
reason: Duplicated business logic causes divergence and maintenance burden; centralize in services.
trade_off: Refactor to centralize business logic; reduces duplication but requires careful cutover and testing.

legacy_element: Derby library inclusion in server.xml (derbyLib fileset)
reason: Bundling DB jars ties deployment to server layout; managed DBs remove the need to ship DB jars.
trade_off: Adjust CI/CD and deployment artifacts; cleaner production deployments.
